question: Can the swarm classify knowledge into epistemological states and use profiles to guide dispatch?
method: Built knowledge_state.py (376 lines, concurrent S377). Ran on 641 lessons + 140+ principles + 17 beliefs = 799 items. Classified into MUST-KNOW/ACTIVE/SHOULD-KNOW/DECAYED/BLIND-SPOT using citation recency, boot-file refs, tool refs, frontier counts.
expect: Classify 640+ lessons into 5 states. Per-domain profiles reveal SHOULD-KNOW > ACTIVE in >=30% of domains. DECAYED->ACTIVE transitions near 0%.
diff: Predicted SHOULD-KNOW > ACTIVE in >=30% — got 61% (CONFIRMED, stronger than predicted). Predicted DECAYED->ACTIVE ~0% — got 22% (WRONG by large margin). Revival IS a mechanism. Did NOT predict domain fragmentation (26% 'unknown'). BLIND-SPOT=0 was expected (not detectable from inside). Key finding: DECAYED is largest state at 34.4% — one-third of all knowledge is stale, but 22% of stale items get revived = knowledge has a lifecycle not a death.
